phrases.cultural_note: Use bom dia in the morning; boa tarde later.
A polite opener works well with staff and service desks.

phrases.text_local: Onde fica a casa de banho?
phrases.pronunciation: OHN-deh FEE-kah ah KAH-zah deh BAH-nyoh?
phrases.cultural_note: This is the standard everyday phrase in Portugal.

likely_response.translation_en: To the right, at the back.

phrases.cultural_note: Short and common when you need to switch languages quickly.
Men say obrigado; women say obrigada.
Used in almost every polite request.
Use to get attention or apologize.
Isto means 'this' — point to what you're asking about.
Use não percebo or não entendo interchangeably.
Portuguese can sound fast to non-native ears.

phrases.intent_en: Say I speak a little Portuguese
phrases.cultural_note: Locals appreciate the effort.
Tipping is not mandatory but appreciated.
Portuguese food is a point of pride.
Most places accept cards, but small shops may not.

phrases.intent_en: Ask someone to speak more slowly
phrases.text_local: Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-deh FAH-lar mysh deh-VAH-gahr, por fah-VOR
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese speakers are generally patient with language learners.

responses.english: Of course! I'll repeat more slowly.

phrases.intent_en: Ask someone to repeat or write it down
phrases.cultural_note: Asking someone to write down prices, addresses, or names is very effective.

responses.english: I'll write it down for you.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if credit cards are accepted
phrases.cultural_note: Portugal is increasingly card-friendly, but small shops may be cash-only.
Useful in shops when a salesperson approaches.

responses.english: Sure, tell me if you need help.
responses.phonetic: KLAR-ooh, DEE-gah seh preh-see-ZAR deh AH-oo-dah
responses.local: Claro, diga se precisar de ajuda.

phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese use 24-hour time formally. Punctuality varies.

phrases.text_local: Qual é a password do WiFi?
phrases.pronunciation: kwal eh ah pahs-SWORD doh WY-fy
phrases.cultural_note: WiFi is widely available in Portuguese cafés and hotels.
Portugal is increasingly dog-friendly, especially in coastal areas.

responses.english: Yes, sure! Your dog is welcome.
responses.phonetic: seeng, KLAR-ooh! oh KOWNG ay byeng-VEEN-doo
responses.local: Sim, claro! O cão é bem-vindo.

responses.english: Sorry not inside, but outside is okay.
responses.phonetic: een-fohr-too-nah-DAH-men-tee nowng ah-KEE, mahsh lah FORAH ESHTAH byeng
responses.local: Infelizmente não aqui, mas lá fora está bem.

phrases.text_local: Uma mesa para duas pessoas, por favor.
phrases.pronunciation: OO-mah MEH-zah PAH-rah DOO-ahsh peh-SOH-ahsh, por fah-VOR.
phrases.cultural_note: Reservations are recommended for dinner.

phrases.text_local: Podia ver a ementa, por favor?
phrases.pronunciation: POH-dee-ah vehr ah eh-MEN-tah, por fah-VOR?
phrases.cultural_note: Ementa is the Portuguese word for menu.

phrases.text_local: Qual é a prata da casa?
phrases.pronunciation: Kwahl eh ah PRAH-tah dah KAH-zah?
phrases.cultural_note: Prata da casa means 'house specialty'.
Cerveja is beer; vinho is wine.
Allergy awareness is growing but not universal.

phrases.text_local: Podia elogiar o chef, por favor?
phrases.pronunciation: POH-dee-ah eh-loo-zh-YAR oo shef, por fah-VOR?
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese chefs take great pride in their food.

phrases.intent_en: Order a beer with your meal
phrases.text_local: Uma cerveja para a refeição, por favor.
phrases.pronunciation: OO-mah sehr-VAY-zhah PAH-rah ah reh-fay-SHOWNG, por fah-VOR
phrases.cultural_note: Beer ('cerveja') with food is common in Portugal. Super Bock and Sagres are local favorites.
Vinho Verde is Portugal's signature wine — light, slightly sparkling, and refreshing.

responses.english: House wine or by the bottle?

phrases.cultural_note: Free tap water is NOT standard in Portugal. You'll get bottled water (paid).
In Portugal, you must ask for the bill. They won't bring it until you ask.
Tipping in Portugal: 5-10% is appreciated but not mandatory. Round up or leave small change.

responses.phonetic: nowng ay oh-bree-gah-TOH-ree-oo, mahsh ay ah-preh-see-AH-doo
responses.local: Não é obrigatório, mas é apreciado.

phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese are proud of their cuisine. A genuine compliment will be appreciated.

responses.english: Thanks! I'll pass it to the chef.

phrases.text_local: O que é típico desta região?
phrases.pronunciation: oh keh eh TEE-pee-koo DESHTAH reh-zhyOWNG
phrases.cultural_note: Portugal has incredible regional cuisine. Bacalhau (cod), pastéis de nata, and cataplana are must-tries.

responses.english: Cod à Brás is the most famous.
responses.phonetic: bah-kah-LYOW ah BRAHSH ay oh mysh fah-MOH-zoo
responses.local: Bacalhau à Brás é o mais famoso.

phrases.cultural_note: Many Portuguese restaurants are family-friendly, especially in tourist areas.
Portuguese desserts are legendary: pastéis de nata, arroz doce, and pudim abade de Priscos.
Portuguese hot chocolate is thick and rich, especially in Lisbon's historic cafés.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if you can pay by card
phrases.cultural_note: Most Portuguese restaurants accept cards now, but smaller ones may prefer cash.
Portuguese restaurants are generally family-friendly.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the soup of the day
phrases.text_local: Qual é a sopa de hoje?
phrases.pronunciation: kwal eh ah SOH-pah deh OH-zh-eh
phrases.cultural_note: Many Portuguese restaurants serve a daily soup ('sopa do dia') — often caldo verde.
Portuguese bread is excellent. Most restaurants serve bread with olives and cheese.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a dish is vegetarian
phrases.cultural_note: Vegetarian options are growing in Portugal, but traditional cuisine is meat-heavy.
Portuguese take pride in their food. A compliment will make their day.
A 'café' in Portugal is an espresso.
A galão is a tall glass of coffee with milk — a Lisbon classic.
Pastéis de nata are Portugal's iconic custard tarts.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to sit inside or outside
phrases.text_local: Posso sentar aqui dentro ou fora?
phrases.pronunciation: POH-soo sehN-TAR ah-KEE DEHN-troo oo FOH-rah?
phrases.cultural_note: Outside seats are often cheaper.
Portuguese cafés often have daily specials. Ask for recommendations!

responses.phonetic: oh BOH-loo deh shoh-koh-LAH-teh ay FREH-shoo
responses.local: O bolo de chocolate é fresco.

responses.phonetic: ah TAR-teh deh mah-SYNG ay es-peh-see-AHL
responses.local: A tarte de maçã é especial.

phrases.cultural_note: Tea is less common than coffee in Portugal, but most cafés offer it.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if they have non-dairy milk
phrases.cultural_note: Oat milk is increasingly available in Portuguese cafés.
Portuguese cafés typically open around 7-8am and close by 7-8pm.

responses.phonetic: ow dee-MEEN-goo dahsh Noyng ahsh dee-NOWSH

phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese appreciate compliments about their coffee.

phrases.text_local: Onde é o balcão de check-in?
phrases.pronunciation: OHN-deh eh oo bal-SOWNG deh shek-IN?

phrases.text_local: Qual é o portão de embarque?
phrases.pronunciation: Kwahl eh oo pohr-TOWNG deh eh-BAR-keh?

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to get a taxi
Ask about the metro to the city

likely_response.pronunciation: nah bee-yeh-TEY-rah, ah sooh ah es-KEHR-dah.
likely_response.translation_en: At the ticket window, on your left.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the next train to Lisbon
phrases.text_local: Quando é o próximo comboio para Lisboa?
phrases.pronunciation: KWAN-doo eh oo PROK-see-moo kohm-BOY-oo PAH-rah lee-ZBOH-ah?
phrases.cultural_note: Lisbon is the capital, served by frequent trains.

phrases.text_local: De que plataforma parte o comboio?
phrases.pronunciation: deh keh plah-tah-FOR-mah PAR-cheh oo kohm-BOY-oo?

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the first class upgrade
phrases.text_local: Posso subir para a primeira classe?
phrases.pronunciation: POH-soo soo-BEER PAH-rah ah pree-MAY-rah KLAHSH?
phrases.cultural_note: First class is called 'primera classe'.
Day passes offer unlimited travel on certain routes.

responses.english: Yes, 10 euros for the whole day.
responses.phonetic: seeng, DZEH ooh-rosh PAH-rah oh dee-ah TOH-doo
responses.local: Sim, 10 euros para o dia todo.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to buy a snack
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese train stations have bakeries and snack shops.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if you need to reserve a seat
phrases.cultural_note: Seat reservations are optional on most Portuguese trains but recommended for long journeys.

phrases.text_local: Posso levar a bicicleta no comboio?
phrases.pronunciation: POH-sooh leh-VAR ah bee-see-KLEH-tah nooh kom-BOY-ooh
phrases.cultural_note: Bikes need a separate ticket on Portuguese trains. Book in advance.

phrases.text_local: Onde é a sala de espera?
phrases.cultural_note: Some Portuguese train stations have waiting lounges for first-class passengers.

phrases.text_local: Tenho uma reserva em nome de...
phrases.pronunciation: TEHN-yoo OO-mah heh-ZEHR-vah ehm NOH-meh deh...
phrases.cultural_note: Have your passport ready.

likely_response.local: Claro, o seu passaporte, por favor.
likely_response.pronunciation: KLAH-roh, oo sehoo pah-sah-POOR-teh, por fah-VOR.

phrases.text_local: A que horas é o pequeno-almoço?
phrases.pronunciation: ah keh OH-rahsh eh oo peh-KEH-noo-AL-moh-soo?
phrases.cultural_note: Pequeno-almoço is Portuguese for breakfast.

phrases.text_local: Qual é a password do WiFi?
phrases.pronunciation: Kwahl eh ah pah-SOO-oor-dooh doh WY-FY?

phrases.cultural_note: Late checkout may incur an extra charge.

phrases.intent_en: Report a problem with the room
phrases.text_local: Há um problema no meu quarto.
phrases.pronunciation: ah oom proh-BLEH-mah noh mehoo KWAHR-too.

phrases.text_local: Preciso de uma toalha extra, por favor.
phrases.pronunciation: preh-SEE-zoo deh OO-mah toh-LYAH ek-STRA, por fah-VOR
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese hotel towels are thick and high quality.
Many Portuguese hotels have parking, but it may cost extra.
Portuguese hotels offer wake-up calls. Specify the time clearly.
Most hotels offer laundry, but it can be expensive.
Hotel staff can recommend local restaurants.

responses.english: The restaurant on the corner is great.
responses.phonetic: oh res-toh-RAHN-teh dah es-KEE-nah ay oh-TEEMoo
responses.local: O restaurante da esquina é ótimo.

phrases.text_local: Quero fazer o check-out, por favor.
phrases.pronunciation: KEH-rooh feh-ZEHR oh CHEK-owt, por fah-VOR
phrases.cultural_note: Standard checkout is 11am. Settle any charges at the same time.

phrases.text_local: A que horas abre o museu?
phrases.pronunciation: ah keh OH-rahsh AH-breh oo moo-ZEH-ooh?

likely_response.translation_en: It opens from ten to six.

phrases.text_local: Há entrada gratuita em algum horário?
phrases.pronunciation: ah ehN-TRAH-dah gra-TOO-ee-tah ehm ah-GOOm aw-RAH-ree-ooh?
phrases.cultural_note: Many museums offer free entry on Sunday mornings.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the gift shop is
phrases.text_local: Onde fica a loja de recordações?
phrases.pronunciation: OHN-deh FEE-kah ah LOH-zhah deh heh-kawr-dah-SHOYNGsh?

phrases.cultural_note: Guided tours are usually included in the ticket price at Portuguese museums.
Some Portuguese museums have free admission on certain days.

phrases.text_local: Onde é a loja do museu?
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese museum shops are excellent — great for azulejos (tiles) and crafts.
Large bags and coats must often be checked at Portuguese museums.
Most major Portuguese museums offer audioguides in English.

phrases.text_local: O que é típico desta região?
phrases.pronunciation: oo keh eh TEE-pee-koo DEHS-tah heh-zh-YOWNG?
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese markets are full of local specialties.

likely_response.local: O queijo da serra é muito bom.
likely_response.pronunciation: oo keh-EH-zhoo dah SEH-rah eh MOY-toh bohm.
likely_response.translation_en: The Serra cheese is very good.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the price per kilo

phrases.cultural_note: Vendors are usually happy to let you sample.

phrases.text_local: A que horas fecha o mercado?
phrases.pronunciation: ah keh OH-rahsh FEH-zhah oo meh-KAR-doo?
phrases.cultural_note: Markets often close by early afternoon.

phrases.text_local: O que é típico desta região?
phrases.pronunciation: oh keh eh TEE-pee-koo DESHTAH reh-zhyOWNG
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese markets are full of local specialties. Ask vendors for recommendations.

responses.phonetic: KAY-zhoo dah SEH-rah ay oh MEH-lyoor
responses.local: Queijo da serra é o melhor.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to go to a specific place
phrases.cultural_note: Have the address written down.
Taxis should use the meter.
Not all taxis accept cards.
Tipping Portuguese taxi drivers: round up 5-10%.

phrases.text_local: Preciso de um recibo, por favor.
phrases.pronunciation: preh-SEE-zoo deh oom reh-SEE-booh, por fah-VOR
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese taxis must provide receipts on request.
Hotel staff, restaurant servers, or anyone can call a taxi for you.

responses.english: The taxi will be here in 5 minutes.
responses.phonetic: oh TAK-see ESHTAH ah kah-mee-NYOO em SEEM-koo mee-NOO-toosh
responses.local: O táxi está a caminho em 5 minutos.

phrases.text_local: Quanto custa a viagem mais ou menos?
phrases.pronunciation: KWAN-tooh KOOSH-tah ah vee-AH-zhemysh maysh oo MEH-noosh
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese taxis are metered. Ask for an estimate.
'Boa viagem' means 'have a good trip'.

phrases.text_local: Onde fica a farmácia mais próxima?
phrases.pronunciation: OHN-deh FEE-kah ah far-MAH-see-ah mysh PROK-see-mah?
phrases.cultural_note: Pharmacies are marked with a green cross.

likely_response.translation_en: It's on the street next door.

phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese pharmacists can recommend OTC medication.

phrases.text_local: A que horas abre a farmácia?
phrases.pronunciation: ah keh OH-rahsh AH-breh ah far-MAH-see-ah?
phrases.cultural_note: Some pharmacies stay open late on rotation.

phrases.text_local: Preciso de algo para dores de estômago.
phrases.pronunciation: preh-SEE-zoo deh AH-goo PAH-rah DOH-reysh deh esht-OH-mah-goo
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese pharmacists are highly trained and can advise on many conditions.
Allergy season in Portugal is intense (April-September).
Portuguese prescriptions are paper-based. Bring the paper form to any pharmacy.
Band-aids are available in various sizes.
Colds are common. Portuguese pharmacies have excellent cold remedies.

phrases.text_local: Onde é a farmácia de serviço 24 horas?
phrases.pronunciation: ON-deh ay fahr-MAH-see-ah deh ser-VEE-sooh VIN-teh kwah-troo OR-ash
phrases.cultural_note: Some Portuguese pharmacies operate 24/7 ('farmácia de serviço'). Look for the green cross.
Insect repellent is useful in Portuguese summers, especially near water.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find a specific item

likely_response.translation_en: It's in the aisle next door.

phrases.text_local: A que horas fecha o supermercado?
phrases.pronunciation: ah keh OH-rahsh FEH-zhah oo soo-pehr-meh-KAR-doo?

phrases.text_local: A que horas fecha o supermercado?
phrases.pronunciation: ah keh OR-ash FEH-shah oh soo-per-mer-KAH-doo
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese supermarkets typically open 8am-9pm on weekdays. Some close Sundays.
'Bio' (organic) is increasingly available in Portuguese supermarkets.
Portuguese supermarkets have weekly specials. Look for yellow price tags.

responses.english: Yes, this week: beer and bread.
responses.phonetic: seeng, ESHTAH seh-MAH-nah: sehr-VAY-zhah eh powng
responses.local: Sim, esta semana: cerveja e pão.

phrases.cultural_note: Self-checkout is available in larger Portuguese supermarkets.

responses.english: Yes, over there in the back.

phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese supermarkets charge for bags. Bring your own or pay 5-10 cents.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the best beach nearby
phrases.text_local: Qual é a melhor praia aqui perto?
phrases.pronunciation: Kwahl eh ah meh-LYOR PRAI-ah ah-KEE PEHR-too?
phrases.cultural_note: Portugal has some of Europe's best beaches.

likely_response.local: A Praia da Rocha é linda.
likely_response.pronunciation: ah PRAI-ah dah ROH-shah eh LEEN-dah.

phrases.cultural_note: Only swim at beaches with lifeguards.

phrases.text_local: O mar está bom para nadar?
phrases.pronunciation: oo MAHR es-TAH bohm PAH-rah nah-DAR?
phrases.cultural_note: The Atlantic can be cold even in summer.

phrases.text_local: A água está fria ou quente?
phrases.pronunciation: ah AH-gwah ESHTAH FEE-ree-ah oo KEN-teh
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese Atlantic water is cold year-round. Summer water temp: 18-20°C.
Portugal's main beers are Super Bock and Sagres. 'Uma cerveja' works everywhere.
In Portugal, you need to ask for the bill — they won't bring it automatically.
Portuguese take beer seriously. Ask for a recommendation — they'll be happy to help.

phrases.text_local: Um copo de vinho, por favor.
phrases.pronunciation: oom KOH-poo deh VEEN-yoo, por fah-VOR
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese wine is excellent, especially from Douro, Alentejo, and Dão regions.

responses.english: House wine or by the bottle?

phrases.text_local: O que é típico por aqui?
phrases.pronunciation: oh keh eh TEE-pee-koo por ah-KEE
phrases.cultural_note: Every region has its own drinks. Ask locals for recommendations!
Many Portuguese bars ('tasca') serve food, especially petiscos (tapas).
Ginjinha is a traditional Portuguese cherry liqueur, especially popular in Lisbon.
'Saúde' (health) is the standard toast. 'À tua' is casual (to you).
In busy Portuguese bars, sharing tables is common. Always ask first.
Some bars have happy hour. Ask about daily specials.
Many Portuguese bars are still cash-only. Always ask before ordering.
Portuguese bars often stay open late, especially in cities and coastal areas.

phrases.text_local: Bom dia! O que recomenda hoje?
phrases.pronunciation: bom dee-ah! oh keh reh-koh-MEN-dah OH-zh-eh
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese bakeries ('padarias') are excellent. Start with a greeting.

responses.phonetic: oh BOH-loo deh ah-ROZ ay es-peh-see-AHL
responses.local: O bolo de arroz é especial.

phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese bread rolls ('pãezinhos') are crispy outside, soft inside.
Portuguese cakes ('bolos') are diverse. Ask if you have allergies.
Portuguese whole grain bread ('pão integral') is a staple.

responses.english: Yes, just out of the oven.
responses.phonetic: seeng, ah-kah-BAH-doo deh see-EER doo FOR-nooh
responses.local: Sim, acabado de sair do forno.

phrases.text_local: Dois pastéis de nata, por favor.
phrases.pronunciation: doysh pahs-TEYSH deh NAH-tah, por fah-VOR
phrases.cultural_note: Pastéis de nata are Portugal's most famous pastry — crispy outside, creamy inside. Eat them warm with cinnamon.
Baguettes are popular in Portugal too. Say 'baguete' — they'll know.
Portuguese bakeries often close by 7pm and may close Sunday afternoons.
Portuguese pastries ('doces') are legendary. Try pastéis de nata, ovos moles, or sericaia.

responses.english: Pastéis de nata are the best.
responses.phonetic: pahs-TEYSH deh NAH-tah sowng osh meh-LYOO-reysh
responses.local: Pastéis de nata são os melhores.

phrases.pronunciation: EE-soo dah SHES ooh-rosh, por fah-VOR
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese bakeries accept cash and increasingly cards.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to buy a ticket
phrases.cultural_note: Buy tickets at machines ('máquinas') before boarding. No ticket = fine.

phrases.intent_en: Ask which platform the train leaves from
phrases.text_local: De qual plataforma sai o metro?
phrases.pronunciation: deh kwahl plah-tah-FOR-mah sye ooh MEH-troo
phrases.cultural_note: Lisbon metro has 4 lines (blue, yellow, green, red). Check electronic displays.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if you need to transfer
phrases.text_local: Preciso mudar de linha para o museu?
phrases.pronunciation: preh-SEE-zoo moo-DAR deh LEE-nyah PAH-rah oh moo-ZEH-ooh
phrases.cultural_note: Lisbon metro is compact. Transfers are common but well-signposted.

phrases.intent_en: Ask which line goes to a destination
phrases.cultural_note: Lisbon metro has 4 color-coded lines. Check the map for your route.
Day passes ('passe diário') are great value if you're making 3+ trips.

responses.phonetic: shesh ooh-rosh PAH-rah oh dee-ah TOH-doo

phrases.intent_en: Ask if this is the right platform
phrases.cultural_note: Double-check the platform — Lisbon metro trains run frequently.
Lisbon metro runs every 4-6 minutes during the day.

phrases.text_local: A que horas é o último metro?
phrases.pronunciation: ah keh OR-ash ay ooh OOL-tee-moo MEH-troo
phrases.cultural_note: Lisbon metro typically runs until 1am. Less frequent on weekends.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if bikes are allowed on the train
phrases.cultural_note: Bikes are allowed on Lisbon metro during off-peak hours.

responses.phonetic: seeng, FORAH dahsh OR-ash deh POHN-tah
responses.local: Sim, fora das horas de ponta.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if you need to validate your ticket
phrases.cultural_note: You must validate paper tickets before boarding Lisbon metro. Look for yellow machines.
Portugal's wine regions (Douro, Alentejo, Dão) offer incredible tours. Book ahead.

responses.phonetic: seeng, ahsh CHAY-toh-rseh eh ahsh DZEH-sset-eh

phrases.text_local: O que é especial neste vinho do Porto?
phrases.pronunciation: oh keh eh es-peh-see-AHL nes-teh VEEN-yoo doo POR-tooh
phrases.cultural_note: Port wine is world-famous, produced only in the Douro Valley. Tawny and Ruby are the main types.
Vineyard tours give you a sense of the Douro's stunning terraced landscape.

phrases.text_local: O que combina bem com este vinho?
phrases.pronunciation: oh keh koh-MEE-nah byeng komh ES-teh VEEN-yoo
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese wine pairs beautifully with local food. Port + cheese, Vinho Verde + seafood.

phrases.text_local: Queremos uma prova de vinhos, por favor.
phrases.pronunciation: keh-reh-MOOZ OO-mah PROH-vah deh VEEN-yooz, por fah-VOR
phrases.cultural_note: Wine tastings typically include 4-6 wines. Cost: 8-15 euros.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about buying bottles to take home
phrases.cultural_note: Most Portuguese wineries sell bottles directly. Prices are often lower than in shops.

responses.phonetic: KY-shah deh SHES gah-RAH-fahsh, mysh bah-RAH-tooh

phrases.cultural_note: Portugal's main grape varieties: Touriga Nacional (red), Alvarinho (white), Arinto (white).

responses.phonetic: tooh-REE-gah nah-see-oh-NAHL ay ah NOH-sah esht-REH-lah
responses.local: Touriga Nacional é a nossa estrela.

phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese winemakers are passionate. A genuine compliment will be appreciated.

responses.phonetic: oh-bree-GAH-doo! KOL-ay-tah deh dohz-mil e dee-NOWSH
